2604.25568.title: Benchmarking bandgap prediction in semiconductors under experimental and realistic evaluation settings
2604.25568.paper_summary: Accurate bandgap prediction is crucial for semiconductor applications, yet machine learning models trained on computational data often struggle to generalize to experimental bandgap measurements. Challenges related to data fidelity, domain generalization, and model interpretability remain insufficiently addressed in existing evaluation frameworks. To bridge this gap, we introduce RealMat-BaG, a benchmark for assessing model reliability under experimentally relevant conditions. We curate an open-access dataset of experimental bandgaps with aligned crystal structures and compare graph neural networks as well as classical machine learning baselines. Our framework evaluates performance across statistical and domain-based splits, examines transfer from DFT-computed to experimental bandgaps, and analyzes interpretability at both elemental-property and structural levels. Our results reveal the fundamental generalization limitations of current bandgap prediction models and establish a benchmark aligned with experimental measurements for developing more reliable learning strategies for materials discovery.
2604.25568.paper_summary_zh: 準確的能隙預測對半導體應用至關重要，但基於計算數據訓練的機器學習模型往往難以泛化到實驗能隙測量上。<lb/>與數據真實性、領域泛化和模型可解釋性相關的挑戰在現有的評估框架中仍然未得到充分解決。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們推出了RealMat-BaG，一個用於評估模型在實驗相關條件下可靠性的基準。<lb/>我們整理了一個開放訪問的實驗能隙數據集，並對齊了晶體結構，並比較了圖神經網絡和傳統機器學習基準。<lb/>我們的框架在統計和基於領域的劃分中評估性能，檢查從DFT計算到實驗能隙的轉移，並在元素性質和結構層面分析可解釋性。<lb/>我們的結果揭示了當前能隙預測模型的基本泛化限制，並建立了一個與實驗測量對齊的基準，以開發更可靠的材料發現學習策略。
2604.25568.author: Haolin Wang et.al.
2604.25568.authors: Haolin Wang, Xianyuan Liu, Anna Jungbluth, Alexandra J. Ramadan, Robert D. J. Oliver, Haiping Lu
